ground_truth.source: Job WHERE status='active' (253); workforce_status WHERE company_id='makro-myanmar-001' AND employment_status='active' (134); performance_duty_templates WHERE company_id='makro-myanmar-001' (16). Read-only, 2026-07-24.
ground_truth.count_note: 253 is the raw active count in Job. The CTO count-contract amendment (2026-07-23) sets clean-public at ~212 after removing synthetic/expired/duplicate rows; this catalog mines the live active set as-written and does not pad or trim.

grade_ladder.note: Rungs proposed from title-keyword frequency across the 253 active listings (Manager 34, Staff 31, Assistant 28, Supervisor 24, Executive 19, Junior 14, Senior 12, Officer 8, Coordinator 6). DIFFERS from the imported US-style ladder Junior/Staff/Senior/Manager/Senior Manager/Department Head in two ways: (1) 'Executive' in Myanmar is a MID-level individual-contributor rung, not a senior leader — it is the single most common mid-title and must be its own rung; (2) 'Supervisor' is a distinct first-line/senior-IC rung (24 listings) that the proposed ladder omits. 'Senior' in Myanmar is a prefix within a grade (Senior Accountant), not a standalone grade. Proposed 6 rungs below reconcile the brief's ladder with market reality.

rungs.name: Junior / Trainee
rungs.scope: Learns the function; contributes to defined tasks under close guidance.
rungs.autonomy: Works to detailed instruction; escalates most decisions.
rungs.decisionRights: None binding; recommends within own task only.
competencies: Follows a documented procedure end-to-end without skipping steps
Asks a clarifying question before acting when a requirement is unclear
Completes assigned tasks within the agreed timeframe
Records own work accurately in the required system/log

rungs.name: Staff / Assistant
rungs.scope: Executes a defined set of recurring tasks for the function independently.
rungs.decisionRights: Decides within established rules (e.g. accepts/rejects an item against a checklist).
rungs.peopleResponsibility: None, or guides a peer on a specific task.
competencies: Executes the core recurring tasks of the role to the documented standard
Identifies and flags an exception or discrepancy instead of passing it on silently
Maintains accurate records for every transaction/task handled
Meets the daily/weekly throughput expected for the role

rungs.name: Officer / Executive
rungs.proposedEquivalent: (proposed ladder had 'Senior' here — market uses Officer/Executive as the mid IC rung)
rungs.scope: Owns a workstream or portfolio within the function; the core mid-level individual contributor.
rungs.autonomy: Plans and executes own workstream; seeks guidance on novel/edge cases.
rungs.decisionRights: Makes routine operational decisions within policy; commits the function on standard matters.
rungs.peopleResponsibility: May coordinate peers or juniors on a task; no formal line reports.
competencies: Owns a workstream end-to-end and delivers it without day-to-day supervision
Analyses a problem and proposes a recommended solution with the trade-offs stated
Coordinates with one other team/function to get a shared outcome
Produces accurate, decision-ready output (report, analysis, code, design) for the workstream
Applies the relevant policy/standard correctly to non-routine cases

rungs.name: Supervisor / Senior
rungs.proposedEquivalent: (NOT a separate rung in the proposed ladder — added because the market uses it heavily: 24 Supervisor + 12 Senior titles)
rungs.scope: Senior individual contributor and/or first-line lead over a small team or shift.
rungs.autonomy: Sets the day-to-day approach for own team/workstream; resolves most operational issues.
rungs.decisionRights: Approves operational decisions within the team; escalates policy/cost exceptions.
rungs.peopleResponsibility: First-line: assigns and checks the work of 2-10 staff; coaches juniors.
competencies: Plans and assigns the daily/weekly work of the team and checks it is done to standard
Resolves an operational problem that a G2/G3 would escalate
Coaches a junior team member to perform a task correctly
Reports team performance and exceptions accurately to the manager
Enforces the relevant standard/safety rule consistently across the team

rungs.name: Manager / Assistant Manager
rungs.scope: Accountable for a function or unit's outcomes, people, and budget within set targets.
rungs.autonomy: Sets objectives and approach for the unit within the department's direction.
rungs.decisionRights: Makes hiring, spend, and priority decisions within delegated limits.
rungs.peopleResponsibility: Manages a team (and/or supervisors); accountable for performance and development.
competencies: Sets clear, measurable objectives for the unit and tracks progress against them
Makes a resourcing/priority trade-off decision within delegated authority
Manages performance: gives feedback, addresses underperformance, develops staff
Owns the unit's budget/cost and delivers within it
Escalates a risk or decision that exceeds delegated authority with a recommendation

rungs.name: Senior Manager / Department Head / Chief
rungs.scope: Leads a department or business unit; sets direction across multiple teams/functions.
rungs.autonomy: Sets department direction and policy within company strategy.
rungs.decisionRights: Approves significant spend, hiring, and policy decisions for the department.
rungs.peopleResponsibility: Leads managers; accountable for the department's capability and results.
competencies: Sets the department's direction and priorities aligned to company strategy
Makes a significant decision (large spend, structure, policy) with the risks weighed
Builds the capability of the managers reporting in
Represents the department to the executive/externally and is accountable for its results
Resolves a cross-department conflict or dependency

duties.duty: Records transactions in the ledger/system within the required cutoff and to the chart of accounts
Reconciles a balance (bank, stock, ledger) and documents the reconciling items each period
Prepares a tax or statutory submission accurately and files it before the deadline
Flags a discrepancy or an out-of-policy entry to the supervisor before posting
Produces a periodic report (P&L, trial balance, AR/AP ageing) that ties out to source records

default_weighting.source: default_weighting on all 16 Makro performance_duty_templates is uniformly duties 70% / ksa 30%

scorecard_rule.assembly: A scorecard = (function duties, weighted 70%) + (grade competencies, weighted 30%). Take the function's duties for the role's department, score each duty 0-5 on observed behaviour, weight by the duty's weighting (equal by default unless the template specifies otherwise), then add the grade competencies for the role's rung (also 0-5). Final = 0.7*duties_score + 0.3*competency_score.
scorecard_rule.hr_override: HR may override per role: (a) re-weight duties vs competencies (e.g. a safety-critical role may weight duties higher); (b) mark a duty non-scoreable / not-applicable for a specific role; (c) add a role-specific duty. Overrides must be recorded on the assignment (weighting + duties jsonb on performance_assignments) with a reason, and never silently change the function default. The 70/30 default applies wherever HR has not overridden.
scorecard_rule.scoring_scale: 0 = not observed / not done; 1-2 = partial / inconsistent; 3 = meets the standard; 4 = consistently exceeds; 5 = exemplary / coaches others.
scorecard_rule.evidence_rule: Each scored duty needs evidence (evidence_types on the template: client_job_jd, manager_sign_off, cco_verify). A duty with no evidence is scored 0 / not-observed, never assumed.

fences.myanmar_text: ALL Myanmar role names/duties are CCO-PENDING. This catalog authors English only. role_type_mm on the 16 templates is client-verbatim or CCO-approved (per template _governance.myanmar_mode); no new Myanmar authored here.
fences.salary: zero salary figures invented or carried. Salary is excluded from the catalog entirely.
fences.market_claims_cited: grade-ladder rungs and title frequencies are cited to the 253-listing title-keyword analysis (see grade_ladder.note).
fences.vague_listings: listings whose title is too generic to score (Supervisor, Senior Supervisor, Senior RS Staff) are flagged in coverage_map.vague_title_flags, NOT silently assigned duties.
fences.unmapped: unmapped listings are reported as findings (coverage_map.unmapped), never defaulted.
fences.out_of_scope: schema, migration, UI are OUT OF SCOPE — they become Codex briefs after KoKo + Z Bo Maung approve this catalog.
